subject: [Pre-read] CB FP&A monthly Initiatives and Update_Apr'26

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find tmr's FP&A monthly meeting material, we will cover following topics


*CB Performance update*

   - Mar’26 Actuals
   - Apr’26 Projections
   - FY26 Status Quo and Scenario Planning


*Key Initiatives Updates : *

   - MP: Compulsory ads impact
   - SLS: Fuel cost impact
   - SBS: FBS Push Direction Change reflects in P&L
   - AI case usage



*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

Slide 9: We have noticed TTS and AE increased their CB logistics rate table
in selected markets. We decided not to follow for now, but will see how the
fuel cost evolve.

Slide 19: What happened to TH in Q3? Suddenly increase seller subsidy %?

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - Can check what FX rates we are using?

And if it is consistent across
   all business?

For 1P + Consignment, I understand that we are negative this
   month for standalone because of FX rates
   - For management EBITDA, why did 1P + Consignment miss by so much?

Slide 7

   - I think if we look at SLS + CNLS, the growth is still lower than
   platform?
Do we have a sense by market how the situation looks?

And for
   CNLS here, do we only take into account Type 1 and 3?
Or also Type 2?

- The same study that we did for VN, we probably need to replicate for
   all markets.
      - FBS/LFF
      - Acquisition of TT only sellers. SLS/LFF
      - CPIGMV study. Whether it make sense to invest more in SLS/LFF/CNLS



Slide 8

   - Have we reconciled TR% between FBS/3PF/SLS for all markets?

Which
   markets are still giving discount and how much?

Slide 10

   - I think we need to do a CPIGMV study vs what market can spend to
   properly align



Slide 12

   - How can utilization be higher than top up?
- We probably need utilization at seller level so RM can action upon?
- Do we track the total in ads escrow?

Do we know if that number is
   increasing/decreasing?
- What are next steps for TH/PH?

Slide 14

   - What amount did we last submit?
Has it changed?

Slide 19

   - Can show market by market view on SLS vs FBS/3PF?

Are there any plans
   to change that?

content.answer:
From JH:

Slide 9:

JH: We have noticed TTS and AE increased their CB logistics rate table in
selected markets. We decided not to follow for now, but will see how the
fuel cost evolve.

Yep


Slide 19:

JH: What happened to TH in Q3? Suddenly increase seller subsidy %?

From CPO perspective, total seller subsidies in Q3’26 remain at 0.26 per
outbound order, while platform voucher rebate per outbound order optimized
across period.

[image: image.png]


From Ian:

Slide 4

Can check what FX rates we are using? And if it is consistent across all
business? For 1P + Consignment, I understand that we are negative this
month for standalone because of FX rates Edric Tan Eng Tong
<edric.tanet@shopee.com> Hao Lei <hao.lei@shopee.com>

reporting)

[image: image.png]

Seafin guideline is to take last day of M-1 (e.g. 28 Feb) as M0 actuals
(Mar) and M+1 projection onwards (Apr - Dec)

Fx rate will be used for reporting across the full month (daily fluctuation
gap borne by Shopee)

Local pays Shopee in LCY

Shopee pays SLS in USD (as per agreed Fx rate in target)

Shopee bears the daily fluctuation risk



For management EBITDA, why did 1P + Consignment miss by so much? Kuan Wai
Mei <waimei.kuan@shopee.com>

contributed from the MP PC2 -313k vs target, with breakdown below:

   -

   -42k from Lovito, mainly driven by lower ads revenue
   -

   -270k from SCS, mainly driven by BR higher order RR, ADO 102%, ADGMV
   105%, while mp pc2 at -27%.


Slide 7

I think if we look at SLS + CNLS, the growth is still lower than platform?
Do we have a sense by market how the situation looks?


And for CNLS here, do we only take into account Type 1 and 3? Or also Type
2? >> includes all


The same study that we did for VN, we probably need to replicate for all
markets.

   -

   FBS/LFF
   -

   Acquisition of TT only sellers. SLS/LFF
   -

   CPIGMV study. Whether it make sense to invest more in SLS/LFF/CNLS

From Ian:

Slide 4

Hi Ian

To give more visibility on the compulsory ads usage, please find the below

1. We track Ads Balance Depletion Ratio (weeks of coverage for ads
balance), and set Jan numbers (before compulsory ads) as target. In March,
the measures got worse, especially for sellers with take rate<5%.
[image: image.png]

2. The summary below outlines the levers we are acting on. The most
impactful ones are: (1) RM KPI for strong RM push as top 10 sellers
contribute to 20% of gap (2) Setting an expiry - we are in discussion with
relevant teams (PM, legal, finance)
[image: image.png]
3. Many of sellers with high balances actually have good  ROAS (benchmarked
agaisnt category ROAS) - we have identify the top sellers and are
pushing for more seller education with both RM push and MKT ads diagnosis
support
[image: image.png]

Best
Leo

content.answer:
Hi Ian

For your questions, pls find answers as follows:

Do we have a sense on what a good ratio would be?

First to clarify the number we are tracking is Ads days of coverage (which
I incorrectly communicated as weeks of coverage previously). Sincerely
apologize!


For CNCB MP total, we take pre compulsory-ads’ number (3-month average Nov’
25-Jan’ 26), as target for end of H1, which is 4.8 days (Mar actual is
8.7). However, this number can be very different across seller group. We
are closely monitoring as sellers ads-spending behaviors shift and plan to
have 7 days as safe line on seller level (given our weekly billing cycle)
once <5%TR sellers’ behavior start to stabilize.


Any sense why we saw a big increase in march for sellers with TR<5%?

It’s mostly due to under-spending of auto escrow - the increase in balance
is faster than the increase in spending, esp. for sellers who used to spend
very little or none. To avoid the snowball effect, we need to ensure
sellers’ net ads take rate above 4% (assuming 80% G2N and full transition
to Tech fee top-up), otherwise the DOC will keep accumulating.


Do we have a sense of overall ads escrow, is it increasing or?

The total top-up has decreased in March - mostly from >5% sellers reduce
their manual top-ups to prioritize consuming existing balance.

[image: image.png]

How do we deal with outliers? There will definitely be sellers who are not
spending and will keep accumulating?

There are a few types of outliers we’ve been monitoring

   -

   Strategic top brands <5% TR, i.e. PPMT and potentially Xiaomi in PH,
   which we have a special approval process to exclude them from auto-escrow
   which ultimately needs JH’s signoff.
   -

   Other managed sellers with hard complaints, we also have a process in
   place for RMs to file escalation, and so far we haven’t heard anything
   -

   For unmanaged sellers, potentially the more concerning ones, actions we
   are work
